title: leucanthemum praecox
intro: Leucanthemum praecox, also known as the Early Daisy or Oxeye Daisy, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its white or yellowish flowers with a yellow center. Leucanthemum praecox has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects due to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Leucanthemum praecox include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Leucanthemum praecox can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, although its effectiveness and safety may vary depending on the individual and the specific preparation method.
